title: Thin Scope, Vertical Slices, and Evidence-Driven Roadmap · Product Reasoning Brief: Story Outcome
summary: A backlog contains many horizontal technical tasks, no complete user path, hidden prerequisites, and no way to demonstrate value until the end. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 story outcome increment in risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.
Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Story Outcome · Theory Story Outcome
steps.instruction:
theory case — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Story Outcome contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Story Outcome · Theory Story Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. For Predict Story Outcome · Theory Story Outcome, keep Story Outcome connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Story Outcome condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GOV.UK Service Manual discovery and alpha guidance — Controls problem-first discovery, affected-user and constraint research, measures of success, riskiest-assumption prototypes, and evidence-based stop decisions before production building.

Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. content.text: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The worked example variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. A defensible solution applies Story Outcome, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-4
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A component or database task is automatically a user story. This fails the Story Outcome contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: GOV.UK Service Manual discovery and alpha guidance (Service Manual current 2026-07-15) — Controls problem-first discovery, affected-user and constraint research, measures of success, riskiest-assumption prototypes, and evidence-based stop decisions before production building.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Story Outcome model · Theory Story Outcome

Write the theory evidence note for Story Outcome: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.
